reasons: Angela can attach to Baxia during a fast engage, improving his durability and chase while he disrupts the enemy formation.
Baxia can enter first while Angela supports his forward position.

proof.summary: Baxia starts fights aggressively, and Angela adds attached support during his entry.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches before Baxia rolls into the fight.
Baxia's team is close enough to follow his disruption.
failureCases: Baxia enters too far ahead of the team.
Angela attaches after Baxia has already absorbed too much damage.
The enemy disengages after Baxia commits.

reasons: Floryn can heal Baxia globally while he rotates, invades, or contests objectives away from the backline.
Baxia's frontline pressure becomes safer when global healing can support his forward plays.

proof.summary: Baxia often fights forward, and Floryn can still support him from anywhere.
worksBestWhen: Floryn times Bloom during Baxia's objective contest.
Baxia uses the heal to stay in front rather than chase too far.
failureCases: Baxia is isolated and focused by burst damage.
Floryn heals before Baxia is pressured.

reasons: Rafaela's healing and movement speed help Baxia keep tempo during rotations and extended frontlining.
Baxia can pressure faster when Rafaela supports his movement between fights.

proof.summary: Baxia pressures the map through movement and durability, while Rafaela supports rotation and sustain.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela stays close enough to speed and heal Baxia before objectives.
Baxia uses the movement support to arrive first, not to overextend alone.
failureCases: Baxia rotates alone without team follow-up.
Rafaela is caught while trying to keep up.
The enemy avoids direct fights and clears waves instead.

reasons: Baxia can occupy the frontline and slow enemy movement while Yve controls a large fight zone from safer range.
Baxia helps hold enemies near the area where Yve wants to apply poke and slow pressure.

proof.summary: Baxia holds space near objectives, and Yve punishes enemies forced to move through that space.
worksBestWhen: Baxia zones the frontline while Yve casts from a safe position.
Yve has vision and protection before using her large zone control.
failureCases: Enemies dive Yve before Baxia can hold the front.
Baxia chases away from Yve's zone.
The team starts the objective before Yve is positioned.

reasons: Baxia can front and reduce enemy sustain value while Lunox supplies the damage needed to finish durable targets.
Lunox benefits when Baxia keeps enemies occupied long enough for her to cast safely.

proof.summary: Baxia forces the fight and weakens sustain-based frontliners, while Lunox follows with magic burst pressure.
worksBestWhen: Baxia keeps enemies occupied long enough for Lunox to free-hit.
Lunox positions safely behind Baxia's frontline pressure.
failureCases: Lunox is forced away before she can follow Baxia's engage.
The enemy collapses on Lunox instead of hitting Baxia.
